records.chapter: The Etiquette of Women in Prayer
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1081 - Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) who said:
"A woman prays in a dress (dir'aa) and a head covering (miqna'a) if it is thick, meaning it is concealing."
records.arabicText: 1081 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: «اَلْمَرْأَةُ تُصَلِّي فِي اَلدِّرْعِ وَ اَلْمِقْنَعَةِ إِذَا كَانَ كَثِيفاً يَعْنِي سَتِيراً.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1081 - Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "A woman prays in a dress (dir'aa) and a head covering (miqna'a) if it is thick, meaning it is concealing."

records.chapter: The Etiquette of Women in Prayer
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1082 - Yunus ibn Ya'qub asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about a man who prays in one garment.
Imam (as) said: "Yes."
He then asked: "What about a woman?"
Imam (as) said: "No, and it is not proper for a free woman (Hurr) to pray when she menstruates except with a khimar (headscarf), unless She does not find it."
records.arabicText: 1082 - وَ سَأَلَ يُونُسُ بْنُ يَعْقُوبَ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يُصَلِّي فِي ثَوْبٍ وَاحِدٍ قَالَ «نَعَمْ» قَالَ قُلْتُ فَالْمَرْأَةُ قَالَ «لاَ وَ لاَ يَصْلُحُ لِلْحُرَّةِ إِذَا حَاضَتْ إِلاَّ اَلْخِمَارُ إِلاَّ أَنْ لاَ تَجِدَهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.1082 - Yunus ibn Ya'qub asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about a man who prays in one garment.
Imam (as) said: "Yes."
He then asked: "What about a woman?"
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "No, and it is not proper for a free woman (Hurr) to pray when she menstruates except with a khimar (headscarf), unless She does not find it."

records.chapter: The Etiquette of Women in Prayer
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1083 - Ali ibn Ja'far asked his brother Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) about a woman who has only one cover (milhafa). How should She pray?
Imam (as) said: "She should wrap herself in it and cover her head, and then pray. If her legs are exposed and She is unable to cover them in any other way, then there is no harm."
records.arabicText: 1083 - وَ سَأَلَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ، أَخَاهُ مُوسَى بْنَ جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلْمَرْأَةِ لَيْسَ لَهَا إِلاَّ مِلْحَفَةٌ وَاحِدَةٌ كَيْفَ تُصَلِّي قَالَ «تَلْتَفُّ فِيهَا وَ تُغَطِّي رَأْسَهَا وَ تُصَلِّي فَإِنْ خَرَجَتْ رِجْلَيْهَا وَ لَيْسَ تَقْدِرُ عَلَى غَيْرِ ذَلِكَ فَلاَ بَأْسَ.

records.chapter: The Etiquette of Women in Prayer
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1084 - In a narration from Al-Mu'alla ibn Khunays, he asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about a woman praying in a shirt (dir') and a cover (milhafa) without a waistcloth (izar) or headcover (miqna'a).
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm if She wraps herself in it, and if it is not wide enough to cover her, She should make it long."
records.arabicText: 1084 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ اَلْمُعَلَّى بْنِ خُنَيْسٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلْمَرْأَةِ تُصَلِّي فِي دِرْعٍ وَ مِلْحَفَةٍ لَيْسَ عَلَيْهَا إِزَارٌ وَ لاَ مِقْنَعَةٌ قَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ إِذَا اِلْتَفَّتْ بِهَا وَ إِنْ لَمْ تَكُنْ تَكْفِيهَا عَرْضاً جَعَلَتْهَا طُولاً.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.1084 - In a narration from Al-Mu'alla ibn Khunays, he asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about a woman praying in a shirt (dir') and a cover (milhafa) without a waistcloth (izar) or headcover (miqna'a).
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no harm if She wraps herself in it, and if it is not wide enough to cover her, She should make it long."

records.chapter: The Etiquette of Women in Prayer
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1085 - Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) who said:
"There is no obligation for a slave woman (amā) to wear a headcover (qina'ah) in prayer, nor for a woman who is 'mukātabah' (a slave who has entered into a contract to pay for her freedom) if her master has not required it as part of the terms of her contract, until She has fulfilled all the terms of her contract. The same rules that apply to a slave in all legal matters apply to her as well."
records.arabicText: 1085 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: «لَيْسَ عَلَى اَلْأَمَةِ قِنَاعٌ فِي اَلصَّلاَةِ وَ لاَ عَلَى اَلْمُدَبَّرَةِ قِنَاعٌ فِي اَلصَّلاَةِ وَ لاَ عَلَى اَلْمُكَاتَبَةِ إِذَا اِشْتَرَطَ عَلَيْهَا مَوْلاَهَا قِنَاعٌ فِي اَلصَّلاَةِ وَ هِيَ مَمْلُوكَةٌ حَتَّى تُؤَدِّيَ جَمِيعَ مُكَاتَبَتِهَا وَ يَجْرِي عَلَيْهَا مَا يَجْرِي عَلَى اَلْمَمْلُوكِ فِي اَلْحُدُودِ كُلِّهَا.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1085 - Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no obligation for a slave woman (amā) to wear a headcover (qina'ah) in prayer, nor for a woman who is 'mukātabah' (a slave who has entered into a contract to pay for her freedom) if her master has not required it as part of the terms of her contract, until She has fulfilled all the terms of her contract. The same rules that apply to a slave in all legal matters apply to her as well."

records.chapter: The Etiquette of Women in Prayer
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1086 - I asked him about a slave woman (amā) who gave birth, and whether the headcover (khimar) would be required for her.
Imam (as) said: "If it were required for her, She would have to wear it when She menstruates, but there is no requirement for her to wear a headcover in prayer."
records.arabicText: 1086 - قَالَ وَ سَأَلْتُهُ: عَنِ اَلْأَمَةِ إِذَا وَلَدَتْ عَلَيْهَا اَلْخِمَارُ قَالَ «لَوْ كَانَ عَلَيْهَا لَكَانَ عَلَيْهَا إِذَا هِيَ حَاضَتْ وَ لَيْسَ عَلَيْهَا اَلتَّقَنُّعُ فِي اَلصَّلاَةِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.1086 - I asked him about a slave woman (amā) who gave birth, and whether the headcover (khimar) would be required for her.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If it were required for her, She would have to wear it when She menstruates, but there is no requirement for her to wear a headcover in prayer."

records.chapter: The Etiquette of Women in Prayer
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1087 - Iyas ibn al-Qasim narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about a man who prays in a woman's izar (waistcloth), in her garment, and uses her khimar (headcover) as a turban.
Imam (as) said: "If She is trustworthy (māʾmūnah), there is no harm."
records.arabicText: 1087 - وَ رَوَى عِيصُ بْنُ اَلْقَاسِمِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: فِي اَلرَّجُلِ يُصَلِّي فِي إِزَارِ اَلْمَرْأَةِ وَ فِي ثَوْبِهَا وَ يَعْتَمُّ بِخِمَارِهَا قَالَ «إِذَا كَانَتْ مَأْمُونَةً فَلاَ بَأْسَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.1087 - Iyas ibn al-Qasim narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about a man who prays in a woman's izar (waistcloth), in her garment, and uses her khimar (headcover) as a turban.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If She is trustworthy (māʾmūnah), there is no harm."

records.chapter: The Etiquette of Women in Prayer
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1088 - It is narrated:
"The best places for women to pray are their homes. A woman's prayer in her house is better than her prayer in a public prayer area (ṣuffah), and her prayer in the public prayer area is better than her prayer in the courtyard of her house. Her prayer in the courtyard is better than her prayer on the roof of her house. And it is disliked for a woman to pray on a roof that is not enclosed (muḥajjar)."
records.arabicText: 1088 - وَ رُوِيَ: «أَنَّ خَيْرَ مَسَاجِدِ اَلنِّسَاءِ اَلْبُيُوتُ وَ صَلاَةَ اَلْمَرْأَةِ فِي بَيْتِهَا أَفْضَلُ مِنْ صَلاَتِهَا فِي صُفَّتِهَا وَ صَلاَتَهَا فِي صُفَّتِهَا أَفْضَلُ مِنْ صَلاَتِهَا فِي صَحْنِ دَارِهَا وَ صَلاَتَهَا فِي صَحْنِ دَارِهَا أَفْضَلُ مِنْ صَلاَتِهَا فِي سَطْحِ بَيْتِهَا وَ تُكْرَهُ لِلْمَرْأَةِ اَلصَّلاَةُ فِي سَطْحٍ غَيْرِ مُحَجَّرٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1088 - It is narrated:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The best places for women to pray are their homes. A woman's prayer in her house is better than her prayer in a public prayer area (ṣuffah), and her prayer in the public prayer area is better than her prayer in the courtyard of her house. Her prayer in the courtyard is better than her prayer on the roof of her house. And it is disliked for a woman to pray on a roof that is not enclosed (muḥajjar)."

records.chapter: The Etiquette of Women in Prayer
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1089 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Do not place women in upper rooms (ghuraf), nor teach them writing, nor teach them Surah Yusuf. Teach them spinning (al-mighzal) and Surah al-Nur."
And when a woman makes the tasbih (glorification of God), She should knot her fingers, for they will be questioned on the Day of Judgment.
records.arabicText:
1089 - وَقَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «لاَ تُنْزِلُوا اَلنِّسَاءَ اَلْغُرَفَ وَ لاَ تُعَلِّمُوهُنَّ اَلْكِتَابَةَ وَ لاَ تُعَلِّمُوهُنَّ سُورَةَ يُوسُفَ وَ عَلِّمُوهُنَّ اَلْمِغْزَلَ وَ سُورَةَ اَلنُّورِ.
فإذا سبحت المرأة عقدت على الأنامل لأنهن مسؤولات يوم القيامة.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1089 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Do not place women in upper rooms (ghuraf), nor teach them writing, nor teach them Surah Yusuf. Teach them spinning (al-mighzal) and Surah al-Nur."
And when a woman makes the tasbih (glorification of God), She should knot her fingers, for they will be questioned on the Day of Judgment.

records.chapter: The Etiquette of Departing From Prayer
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1090 - Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) who said:
"When you finish your prayer, turn to your right as you leave."
records.arabicText: 1090 - رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: «إِذَا اِنْصَرَفْتَ مِنَ اَلصَّلاَةِ فَانْصَرِفْ عَنْ يَمِينِكَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1090 - Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "When you finish your prayer, turn to your right as you leave."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Allah (swt), Blessed and Exalted, says: "And establish the prayer and give the zakah and bow with those who bow." (Surah Al-Baqarah, 2:43).
Allah (swt) has commanded the congregation as He commanded the prayer. Allah (swt), Blessed and Exalted, has made it obligatory upon the people to pray five times a day, from Jumu’ah to Jumu’ah, totaling thirty-five prayers. Among them, one prayer is obligatory in congregation, which is the Jumu’ah prayer. As for the other prayers, it is not obligatory to pray in congregation, but it is a recommended practice (Sunnah).
Whoever neglects it without a valid excuse has no prayer. Whoever neglects three consecutive Jumu’ah prayers without a valid excuse is considered a hypocrite. A man's prayer in congregation is worth twenty-five degrees more than his prayer alone in Paradise. The prayer in congregation is worth twenty-four prayers more than the individual prayer, making a total of twenty-five prayers.
records.arabicText:
قال الله تبارك وتعالى: " وأقيموا الصلاة وآتوا الزكاة واركعوا مع الراكعين "
فأمر الله بالجماعة كما أمر بالصلاة، وفرض الله تبارك وتعالى على الناس من الجمعة إلى الجمعة خمسا وثلاثين صلاة، فيها صلاة واحدة فرضها الله في جماعة وهي الجمعة فأما سائر الصلوات فليس الاجتماع إليها بمفروض ولكنه سنة، من تركها رغبة عنها وعن جماعة المسلمين من غير علة فلا صلاة له ومن ترك ثلاث جمعات متواليات من غير علة فهو منافق وصلاة الرجل في جماعة تفضل على صلاة الرجل وحده بخمس وعشرين درجة في الجنة، والصلاة في الجماعة تفضل صلاة الفرد بأربع وعشرين صلاة فيكون خمسا وعشرين صلاة.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1091 - Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated that Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
"There is no prayer for the one who does not attend the congregational prayer from among the neighbors of the mosque, except for someone who is ill or preoccupied."
records.arabicText: 1091 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: «لاَ صَلاَةَ لِمَنْ لاَ يَشْهَدُ اَلصَّلاَةَ مِنْ جِيرَانِ اَلْمَسْجِدِ إِلاَّ مَرِيضٌ أَوْ مَشْغُولٌ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1091 - Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated that Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no prayer for the one who does not attend the congregational prayer from among the neighbors of the mosque, except for someone who is ill or preoccupied."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1092 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said to a group of people:
"You must attend the mosque, or I will burn down your houses."
records.arabicText: 1092 - وَقَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ لِقَوْمٍ: «لَتَحْضُرُنَّ اَلْمَسْجِدَ أَوْ لَأُحْرِقَنَّ عَلَيْكُمْ مَنَازِلَكُمْ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1092 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said to a group of people:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "You must attend the mosque, or I will burn down your houses."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1093 – He (as) said:
"Whoever prays the five daily prayers in congregation, assume every good about him."
records.arabicText: 1093 - وَقَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «مَنْ صَلَّى اَلصَّلَوَاتِ اَلْخَمْسَ جَمَاعَةً فَظُنُّوا بِهِ كُلَّ خَيْرٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1093 – He (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever prays the five daily prayers in congregation, assume every good about him."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.1094 - Imam (as) said: "Two people constitute a congregation."
records.arabicText: 1094 - وَقَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «اَلاِثْنَانِ جَمَاعَةٌ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1094 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Two people constitute a congregation."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1095 - Al-Hasan al-Sayqal asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about the minimum number for a congregation.
Imam (as) said: "A man and a woman."
records.arabicText: 1095 - وَ سَأَلَ اَلْحَسَنُ اَلصَّيْقَلُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنْ أَقَلِّ مَا تَكُونُ اَلْجَمَاعَةُ قَالَ «رَجُلٌ وَ اِمْرَأَةٌ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1095 - Al-Hasan al-Sayqal asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about the minimum number for a congregation.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "A man and a woman."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1096 - The Prophet said:
"A believer alone is a proof (Hujjah), and a believer alone is a congregation."
records.arabicText: 1096 - وَ قَدْ قَالَ اَلنَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ: «اَلْمُؤْمِنُ وَحْدَهُ حُجَّةٌ وَ اَلْمُؤْمِنُ وَحْدَهُ جَمَاعَةٌ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1096 - The Prophet said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "A believer alone is a proof (Hujjah), and a believer alone is a congregation."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1097 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) prayed the Fajr (dawn) prayer one day, and after finishing, he turned his face towards his companions and asked about certain people by name, asking if they were present for the prayer.
They replied: "No, O’ Messenger of Allah (sw)."
He (sw) then said: "Are they absent?"
They said: "No, O’ Messenger of Allah (sw)."
He (sw) said: "Indeed, there is no prayer heavier on the hypocrites than this prayer (Fajr) and the 'Isha' (night) prayer. If they knew the reward that is in them, they would come to them, even if crawling."
records.arabicText: 1097 - وَ صَلَّى رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ اَلْفَجْرَ ذَاتَ يَوْمٍ فَلَمَّا اِنْصَرَفَ أَقْبَلَ بِوَجْهِهِ عَلَى أَصْحَابِهِ فَسَأَلَ عَنْ أُنَاسٍ يُسَمِّيهِمْ بِأَسْمَائِهِمْ هَلْ حَضَرُوا اَلصَّلاَةَ قَالُوا لاَ يَا رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ فَقَالَ «غُيَّبٌ هُمْ » فَقَالُوا لاَ يَا رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ قَالَ «أَمَا إِنَّهُ لَيْسَ مِنْ صَلاَةٍ أَثْقَلَ عَلَى اَلْمُنَافِقِينَ مِنْ هَذِهِ اَلصَّلاَةِ وَ صَلاَةِ اَلْعِشَاءِ اَلْآخِرَةِ وَ لَوْ عَلِمُوا اَلْفَضْلَ اَلَّذِي فِيهِمَا لَأَتَوْهُمَا وَ لَوْ حَبْواً.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1097 -
records.thaqalaynMatn:
The Messenger of Allah (sw) prayed the Fajr (dawn) prayer one day, and after finishing, he turned his face towards his companions and asked about certain people by name, asking if they were present for the prayer.
They replied: "No, O’ Messenger of Allah (sw)."
He (sw) then said: "Are they absent?"
They said: "No, O’ Messenger of Allah (sw)."
He (sw) said: "Indeed, there is no prayer heavier on the hypocrites than this prayer (Fajr) and the 'Isha' (night) prayer. If they knew the reward that is in them, they would come to them, even if crawling."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1098 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Whoever prays the morning (Fajr) and the evening ('Isha') prayers in congregation is under the protection of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic. Whoever wrongs him, wrongs Allah (swt), and whoever belittles him, belittles Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic."
records.arabicText: 1098 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «مَنْ صَلَّى اَلْغَدَاةَ وَ اَلْعِشَاءَ اَلْآخِرَةَ فِي جَمَاعَةٍ فَهُوَ فِي ذِمَّةِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ وَ مَنْ ظَلَمَهُ فَإِنَّمَا يَظْلِمُ اَللَّهَ وَ مَنْ حَقَّرَهُ فَإِنَّمَا يُحَقِّرُ اَللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1098 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever prays the morning (Fajr) and the evening ('Isha') prayers in congregation is under the protection of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic. Whoever wrongs him, wrongs Allah (swt), and whoever belittles him, belittles Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1099 - The Prophet (sw) said:
"If the sandals are wet, then pray in your dwellings."
records.arabicText: 1099 - لِقَوْلِ اَلنَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ: «إِذَا اِبْتَلَّتِ اَلنِّعَالُ فَالصَّلاَةُ فِي اَلرِّحَالِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1099 - The Prophet (sw) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If the sandals are wet, then pray in your dwellings."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1100 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said:
"The Imam of the people is their representative, so choose the best among you to lead."
records.arabicText: 1100 - وَقَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ: «إِمَامُ اَلْقَوْمِ وَافِدُهُمْ فَقَدِّمُوا أَفْضَلَكُمْ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1100 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The Imam of the people is their representative, so choose the best among you to lead."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1110 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Three people should not be prayed behind: the one who is unknown (al-majhūl), the extremist (al-ghālī) even if he claims to hold your beliefs, and the one who openly commits sinful acts (al-mujāhir bil-fisq) even if he appears moderate."
records.arabicText: 1110 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «ثَلاَثَةٌ لاَ يُصَلَّى خَلْفَهُمُ اَلْمَجْهُولُ وَ اَلْغَالِي وَ إِنْ كَانَ يَقُولُ بِقَوْلِكَ وَ اَلْمُجَاهِرُ بِالْفِسْقِ وَ إِنْ كَانَ مُقْتَصِداً.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1110 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Three people should not be prayed behind: the one who is unknown (al-majhūl), the extremist (al-ghālī) even if he claims to hold your beliefs, and the one who openly commits sinful acts (al-mujāhir bil-fisq) even if he appears moderate."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1101 - Imam (as) said:
"If it pleases you to purify and perfect your prayers, then choose the best among you (to lead)."
records.arabicText: 1101 - وَقَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «إِنْ سَرَّكُمْ أَنْ تُزَكُّوا صَلاَتَكُمْ فَقَدِّمُوا خِيَارَكُمْ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1101 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If it pleases you to purify and perfect your prayers, then choose the best among you (to lead)."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1102 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said:
"Whoever leads a people in prayer while there is someone among them who is more knowledgeable than him, their affairs will continue to decline until the Day of Judgment."
records.arabicText: 1102 - وَقَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ: «مَنْ صَلَّى بِقَوْمٍ وَ فِيهِمْ مَنْ هُوَ أَعْلَمُ مِنْهُ لَمْ يَزَلْ أَمْرُهُمْ إِلَى سَفَالٍ إِلَى يَوْمِ اَلْقِيَامَةِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1102 -
records.thaqalaynMatn:
The Messenger of Allah (sw) said:
"Whoever leads a people in prayer while there is someone among them who is more knowledgeable than him, their affairs will continue to decline until the Day of Judgment."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1103 - Al-Hussain ibn Kathir narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that a man asked him about reciting (Qur'an) behind the Imam (during prayer).
Imam (as) said: "No, the Imam is responsible for the recitation, but the Imam does not take responsibility for the prayers of those behind him; he is only responsible for the recitation."
records.arabicText: 1103 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحُسَيْنُ بْنُ كَثِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: أَنَّهُ سَأَلَهُ رَجُلٌ عَنِ اَلْقِرَاءَةِ خَلْفَ اَلْإِمَامِ فَقَالَ «لاَ إِنَّ اَلْإِمَامَ ضَامِنٌ لِلْقِرَاءَةِ وَ لَيْسَ يَضْمَنُ اَلْإِمَامُ صَلاَةَ اَلَّذِينَ هُمْ مِنْ خَلْفِهِ إِنَّمَا يَضْمَنُ اَلْقِرَاءَةَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1103 - Al-Hussain ibn Kathir narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that a man asked him about reciting (Qur'an) behind the Imam (during prayer).
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "No, the Imam is responsible for the recitation, but the Imam does not take responsibility for the prayers of those behind him; he is only responsible for the recitation."
